result:
I’d make it impossible for people to be certain they’re right.

Not confused, not ignorant—just permanently aware that they might be wrong. Even a tiny, constant doubt baked into everyone’s thinking.

A lot of the worst things people do—cruelty, fanaticism, corruption, even everyday stubbornness—come from overconfidence in their own view of the world. If that certainty softened, even a little, people would listen more, double-check themselves, and treat others less like enemies and more like… people who might know something they don’t.

You’d still get disagreement, ambition, creativity—all the good stuff. But with less arrogance driving harm.

It wouldn’t fix everything. But it would quietly improve almost everything.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
